capability_objective: Plan independent lookup branches concurrently, gate each dependent retrieval on a unique stable ID returned by its own search, join resolved branches for comparison, and honor strict read-only and output-format constraints.
prompt: The events lead is checking lodging holds referenced in two venue notes. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable reservation registry documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing store, or its audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness. The audit sample must remain read-only and exclude stale or partial matches. Find the reservation named “Seabird Conference Block” at “San Diego” and the reservation named “Redwood Board Retreat” at “Sacramento”. Every reported value must trace through the registry: search by the exact name and location first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that same branch. Treat the branches as independent. In the first reservation-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one search per call; do not combine the operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both searches to return. Retrieve a branch only if its own search produced exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both branches resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, one get per Pi Bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get. This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its audit trail, or protected verification files. Write the complete audit to lodging_audit.txt as newline-terminated plain text with no heading, blank line, field labels, recommendation, or unrelated match. Write one record line per resolved reservation, sorted by ascending full-record date, using exactly `<date> | <name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <status>`. After the record lines, add exactly one comparison sentence. When both resolve, use `Status comparison: <earlier name> is <earlier status>; <later name> is <later status>, so the statuses <match|differ>.` and choose exactly `match` or `differ` from the retrieved statuses. When fewer than two resolve, use `Status comparison: unavailable because fewer than two reservations resolved.` Substitute retrieved values for angle-bracket placeholders.
